LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RADIUS INTELLIGENCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT RADIUS INTELLIGENCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF COLE RATIAS IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating, Issue, Evidentiary, Monetary Sanctions And/Or Contempt For Violation Of Court Order
Off Calendar Jun-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF COLE RATIAS' Motion For Terminating, Issue, Evidentiary, Monetary Sanctions And/Or Contempt For Violation Of Court Order:

Motion for terminating sanctions GRANTED. No timely opposition filed. Based on the record presented, Defendant failed to comply with the Court's prior order to respond to discovery, despite the closure of the bankruptcy proceeding and requests by Plaintiff for Defendant to respond to the Court's order. Thus, there is a history of failures to comply with discovery or this Court's orders, and there is no evidence that would show less severe sanctions would produce compliance with discovery. See Moofly Productions LLC v. Favila (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 1, 10-12 (collecting cases). Defendant's answer is STRICKEN. CCP 2023.030(d)(1). Plaintiff should submit Form Civ 100. The request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of 3,812.50, payable no later than July 7, 2022.

Plaintiff's counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling via email to sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: A. Datt, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Plaintiff Cole Ratias' Motion To Compel Defendant To Serve Discovery Responses, Produce Documents, Appear For Deposition And Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Cole Ratias' Motion To Compel Defendant To Serve Discovery Responses, Produce Documents, Appear For Deposition And Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Cole Ratias's "motion to compel defendant to (1) serve responses to plaintiff's form interrogatories, set one, without objections; (2) serve responses to plaintiff's requests for production of documents, set one, without objections; (3) produce all responsive documents; (4) appear for deposition; and (5) pay monetary sanctions" is granted. The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is adopted.
The responses to interrogatories and requests for production of documents shall be served on or before August 4, 2021. The documents shall be produced on or before August 11. The deposition shall occur on or before August 18. The requested $7,500 in sanctions is awarded.

Because defendant's counsel only recently appeared in the case, the sanctions are against defendant only and shall be paid by August 18.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT RADIUS INTELLIGENCE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant Radius Intelligence, Inc.'s demurrer to all three causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Cole Ratias is overruled as to all three causes of action. Radius' request to take judicial notice of the 2009 separation agreement is denied because the parties dispute the validity and binding nature of that document. Mr. Ratias contends that the 2010 release superseded all prior contracts and Radius implicitly asserts that the 2009 separation agreement is binding. "[W]e hold the existence of a contract between private parties cannot be established by judicial notice under Evidence Code section 452, subdivision (h). Judicial notice under Evidence Code section 452, subdivision (h) is intended to cover facts which are not reasonably subject to dispute and are easily verified." (Gould v. Maryland Sound Industries, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1137, 1145.) The judicial notice cases relied on by Radius are distinguishable because Mr. Ratias does not expressly refer to the 2009 agreement in the complaint. Mr. Ratias states a claim for breach of contract. A demurrer to a cause of action for breach of contract admits not only the contents of the contract, but also any pleaded meaning which the contract is reasonably susceptible. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) Radius fails to show that Mr. Ratias' ascribed meaning is unreasonable and the recitals are not irrelevant to the interpretation process. (Golden West Baseball Co. v. City of Anaheim (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 11, 37-38 (provision of lease was not "'mere' recital," and even if it was, resort to it was necessary to interpret covenants)). The face of the complaint does not necessarily show that any of the claims are barred by either the statute of limitations or laches. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 =302/hk
